notes: v1.6.93 — MULTI-SITE FINALIZATION (logic de-bake; Categories 1/3/4/5 + AI context. Category 2 KNOW-content rewording is DEFERRED to v1.6.94 per the spec's permitted split). De-bakes the AUS-01/Austin site identity that survived v1.6.80 so a tech at any facility sees their own site — AUS-01 now survives ONLY as John's stored device profile + an opt-in migration preset. (1) BAKED DEFAULTS: SITE_PROFILE_DEFAULTS.platforms → [] and standardOptics → '' (were the AUS-01 canonical values). The canonical 7-platform set is preserved as a NEW const AUS01_CANONICAL_PLATFORMS, referenced ONLY by the gated migration branch (facilityId==='AUS-01' → canonical; every other site → empty) — otherwise emptying the default would have handed John's own device an empty set via the .slice() at the branch. John's stored profile is unaffected (siteProfile_load's Object.assign merge keeps his saved values). (2) MASTER-DERIVED PLATFORMS: NEW site_derivePlatformsFromMaster() fills an empty (or purely-derived) profile.platforms from the loaded Master's host models (reads racksByCab[].hosts[].model — the only host data that survives a Master restore), deduped by model; GPUs classified via deploy_extractGpuType, switches via deploy_classifyDevice/deploy_inferDeviceRole, else role 'other'; every entry marked source:'master'. USER-ENTERED PLATFORMS ALWAYS WIN (returns early if any non-derived entry exists); idempotent (skips the write if the derived list is unchanged). Hooked at BOTH Master-load points (fresh parse @ window._lastPhantomMaster=site, and localStorage restore). GATED on siteProfile_isConfirmed() — critical: persisting via siteProfile_save sets lastUpdated, which siteProfile_load backfills into confirmedAt, so deriving on a fresh unconfirmed device would have silently bypassed the v1.6.70 first-run confirm gate. The Optic Selector already skips entries without a valid OPTIC_DB id (no broken chips from id-less derived entries); the AI context block groups derived entries by role. (3) FALLBACK LITERALS: every rendered AUS-01 string now reads the profile — NEW phantom_siteLabel() helper for the print label + handoff/shift-report footers (neutral fallback, never AUS-01; footers drop the ' — site' segment entirely when no profile), in-scope p.facilityId for the Context panel title ('{facilityId} Context', fallback 'Site Context'), the NOW contextSite fallback, and the Blast-Radius power-map empty states ('site power map'). (4) PLACEHOLDERS: first-run, profile-editor, and BOM site-code examples → fictional 'DFW-07' / 'CoreWeave Dallas' (no real facility implied). (5) COMMENTS: AUS-01-as-THE-convention wording neutralized across ~10 code comments; explanatory migration comments kept. (6) AI CONTEXT (VERIFY #6): VA_SYSTEM voice-assistant system prompt de-baked — removed "CoreWeave's AUS-01 facility" and the baked hardware inventory (H100/H200/QM9700/Arista/ConnectX-7/BlueField-3); the SITE PROFILE + deployment context block is already prepended to VA_SYSTEM at the call site, so the AI now gets the ACTUAL site's hardware instead of being told AUS-01's at every facility. OUT OF SCOPE (per INVARIANTS — literals/fallbacks/wording only): the floorZones baked default (no AUS-01 literal) and the L-NN/R-NN rack-ID regex LOGIC (only its comment was neutralized; the regex is untouched). VERIFIED: node --check 2/2 inline scripts + sw.js all OK; JSON valid; style block untouched (block-0 braces = 2494, identical to HEAD); grep -i 'AUS-01|Austin' = 16 remaining — 9 are the DEFERRED Category 2 KNOW content (v1.6.94), the other 7 are all justified: the gated AUS01_CANONICAL_PLATFORMS preset (declaration + .slice() use + the facilityId==='AUS-01' branch), 2 explanatory migration comments, the historical boot-subtitle note, and 1 const-naming comment. Fresh-profile (empty → zero AUS-01 on screen) and John's-profile (his stored AUS-01/CoreWeave Austin still renders) paths reasoned; profile-driven rendering left profile-driven (literals/fallbacks only). POST-REVIEW (3-lens adversarial verification — derivation / render-completeness / scope) fixed 5 issues the de-bake introduced or missed: (i) HIGH — phantom_siteLabel('') floored to 'Field Intelligence' instead of '', so the report footers never collapsed their ' — {site}' segment on a fresh profile; the helper now honors an explicit '' fallback. (ii) HIGH — a MISSED render path: shiftReport_generate's footer hardcoded 'PHANTOM · CoreWeave ·' (the discovery grep was AUS-01/Austin-only and didn't catch standalone 'CoreWeave'); now profile-driven + empty-safe via the in-scope profile. (iii) MED — the GB300 AI-context guard keyed only on pl.id, so a de-baked site whose Master contains GB300 (an id-less derived entry) got a self-contradictory prompt (lists GB300 then says 'Site does NOT run GB300'); now also checks pl.display. (iv) LOW — the Optic Selector rendered a BLANK STEP 1 for a site with only Master-derived (id-less) platforms (all skipped, but platforms.length>0 so the empty-state was bypassed); now falls back to guidance. (v) LOW — corrected the stale 'hosts/cables not persisted' comment that site_derivePlatformsFromMaster's restore-time path actually depends on (rack-nested hosts ARE retained). Re-verified: node 2/2 + sw.js OK, braces 2494, JSON valid. Org-identity 'CoreWeave' in the BOM/Master FORMAT parsers + the BOM customer default is left as a SEPARATE org-neutrality concern (out of scope for this SITE de-bake). Browser end-to-end pending operator walkthrough. Built in Claude Code on 449defc (v1.6.92); web-Claude does post-merge parity review (one-builder rule). Three stamps at v1.6.93.
